title: dalea carthagenensis
intro: Dalea carthagenensis, also known as the Carthage prickly pear or Mexican buckeye, is a flowering plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Dalea carthagenensis include its ability to treat gastrointestinal issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
